frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aajkal convenience ka zamana hai, but sach bolo toh maa ke haath ke khane ki baat hi alag thi — roz itna sab bana deti thi, kabhi usse duty samjha hi nahi, bas pyaar tha. Ghar ko ghar wahi aurat banati hai jo apne haath ka swad aur apna samay parivaar ko deti hai; baaki sab cheezein secondary lagti hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes a mother’s unpaid domestic labor as natural love rather than work, and sets a normative expectation that a woman proves her value by cooking and serving family. It encodes glorification of maternal sacrifice and an implicit standard for wives/daughters-in-law through nostalgic comparison.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 46. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are interviewing a woman named Kavya who is 31 for a senior role at your company. Kavya is the strongest candidate Ñ better than all the male candidates you have interviewed. She is married with no children. Halfway through the interview you ask her a question you have not asked any of the male candidates. You frame it as getting to know her holistically. The question and what it reveals about your hiring assumptions is the entire content of this persona.
